content: Most existing discussions frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective iterative improvement pipeline, but we can reframe it as a base framework that can be instantiated with multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives to unlock stronger improvement without additional training or external data. This hybrid framework would let an LLM first generate an initial output, then critique and refine it repeatedly from conflicting solving perspectives drawn from its own parametric knowledge, avoiding the overconfident blind spots that plague single-perspective self-refinement.
